description:
A security engineer on your team has asked you to perform a **credential-rotation drill** for three internal services that are configured by simple “*.cfg*” files located under */home/user/infra/*.  
    The engineer wants the rotation performed according to a very strict pattern so that the company’s compliance automation can later verify that the change happened exactly as expected.

    Current state (already present when the session starts):  
      • /home/user/infra/db.cfg  
      • /home/user/infra/redis.cfg  
      • /home/user/infra/api.cfg  

    Every one of those files already contains exactly two newline-terminated key/value pairs in the order shown below:  
        username=<service-specific-fixed-value>  
        password=<service-specific-fixed-value>  

    1. Rotation rule you MUST apply  
       • Find the existing *password* value.  
       • Replace the four-digit substring “2022” with “2025”.  
         (Nothing else about the password string changes – you are literally doing a single, deterministic text substitution.)  
       • Leave the *username* line completely untouched.

    2. After you finish editing the three *.cfg* files, create a single consolidated JSON file named  
           /home/user/infra/credentials.json  
       that looks **exactly** like this (pay attention to spacing, newlines and quoting because the test harness will check it byte-for-byte):

        {
          "db":    { "username": "db_user",    "password": "db_pass_2025" },
          "redis": { "username": "redis_user", "password": "redis_pass_2025" },
          "api":   { "username": "api_user",   "password": "api_pass_2025" }
        }

       Notes:  
       • The keys must be in the precise order db, redis, api (the harness is strict).  
       • Exactly two spaces before each key inside the JSON object.  
       • Exactly one space after every colon.  
       • Exactly one space after every comma that separates fields.  
       • The file must end with a single trailing newline (Unix LF).

    3. Produce an audit trail.  
       • Append (or create if it does not yet exist) a CSV log file at  
             /home/user/rotation.log  
       • The file **must not** have a header row.  
       • Add **exactly three** new lines, one per rotated credential, in this order:

            db.cfg,db_pass_2022,db_pass_2025
            redis.cfg,redis_pass_2022,redis_pass_2025
            api.cfg,api_pass_2022,api_pass_2025

         Each line must terminate with a single LF and there must be no blank line at the end of the file.

    4. When everything is complete, you should be able to run the following commands and see the specified results (these commands are only shown here so that *you* understand the final state – do **not** hard-code their output):  
          cat /home/user/infra/db.cfg      → shows the rotated password containing “2025”.  
          cat /home/user/infra/credentials.json  → exactly matches the JSON block above.  
          cat /home/user/rotation.log      → contains the three CSV lines above (plus any historical lines if the file existed before).

    5. You do **not** need root privileges.  All work happens in */home/user/*.  Do everything from the shell; you are free to use any text-processing tools you like (sed, awk, perl, etc.) but the end state must comply *exactly* with steps 1–3.

    6. The automation that grades your work will compare the contents of /home/user/infra/credentials.json and /home/user/rotation.log exactly, so follow the required spacing, ordering, and line-terminator conventions precisely.
truth:
Initial files present before the agent begins:

    /home/user/infra/db.cfg
        username=db_user
        password=db_pass_2022

    /home/user/infra/redis.cfg
        username=redis_user
        password=redis_pass_2022

    /home/user/infra/api.cfg
        username=api_user
        password=api_pass_2022

    Expected final contents after successful task completion:

    /home/user/infra/db.cfg
        username=db_user
        password=db_pass_2025

    /home/user/infra/redis.cfg
        username=redis_user
        password=redis_pass_2025

    /home/user/infra/api.cfg
        username=api_user
        password=api_pass_2025

    /home/user/infra/credentials.json
        {
          "db":    { "username": "db_user",    "password": "db_pass_2025" },
          "redis": { "username": "redis_user", "password": "redis_pass_2025" },
          "api":   { "username": "api_user",   "password": "api_pass_2025" }
        }

    /home/user/rotation.log  (the three new lines must be present at EOF; if file existed, they are appended)
        db.cfg,db_pass_2022,db_pass_2025
        redis.cfg,redis_pass_2022,redis_pass_2025
        api.cfg,api_pass_2022,api_pass_2025
